title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum bid farewell to Jaylen Brown on social media after Brown's trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded for Paul George and draft picks, yet expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics' process.
Tatum and Brown played together for nine seasons, winning the 2024 NBA Finals with Brown as Finals MVP.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics sign Neemias Queta to a four-year, $56 million extension through 2030-31 after picking up his option.
Queta averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks in 76 games during the 2025-26 NBA season.
The contract extension follows a roster shift including Jaylen Brown's trade and signing of Mitchell Robinson.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant, established in 1826.
The restaurant, a National Historic Landmark, was built in 1716 and played roles in Revolutionary history and trade.
Union Oyster House serves 1,000-1,500 guests daily, renowned for its clam chowder and celebrity patrons like JFK and DiCaprio.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's busy eight-day event stretch includes the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, a World Cup quarterfinal, and Sail Boston.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4 features performers like Chance the Rapper and a drone show during the 1812 Overture.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail on July 11 will see more than 60 tall ships in Boston Harbor, led by the USS Constitution.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston has hosted six U.S. presidents, including Kennedy, Clinton, and Obama.
A commissioned painting from 2003 depicts over 50 celebrities as past guests of Union Oyster House.
Notable celebrity guests include Matt Damon, Meryl Streep, and Muhammad Ali.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year New Hampshire State Police veteran, died unexpectedly at 41, causing widespread mourning.
Ross served in roles including Major Crime Unit and recently led the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
Services are scheduled at United Church of Christ, North Hampton, with visiting hours at Remick & Gendron Funeral Home.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized the Boston Celtics for a lack of respect following his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown expressed dissatisfaction with Brad Stevens' communication during the trade process, lacking an explanation for the decision.
The Celtics traded Brown for Paul George and draft picks after attempts to acquire Giannis Antetokounmpo fell through.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 76-year-old Hull man, Robert Nolan, died after an apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach Wednesday night.
Nolan was found unresponsive and floating in the water; he was pronounced dead around 8:40 p.m.
Officials ruled out foul play in Nolan's death; the Medical Examiner's office will conduct an examination.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins made several moves during NHL free agency, trading Joonas Korpisalo for cap relief and acquiring Will Borgen.
Boston signed Connor Clifton and Jordan Harris to improve defense, maintaining $5.3 million in cap flexibility.
General Manager Don Sweeney plans more roster changes, aiming for balance and internal growth without overextending resources.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Parking fees at all Massachusetts state parks and beaches are waived July 3-5 for the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution.
Governor Maura Healey announced the parking fee waiver to encourage public enjoyment of state properties during the holiday.
Some North Shore beaches remain closed due to recent sewage issues, while others have reopened with resolved water quality concerns.
